title: einsum GPU: 2-input equations with operand repeats (e.g. 'ii,j->j')
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Bugfix Batch 6 / Dispatch A2 (#822) extended einsum_two_gpu with a general permute+reshape+bmm decomposition for multi-axis and permuted contractions. Equations where either operand has repeated input indices (e.g. 'ii,j->j', 'ij,jj->i') still return Err(NotImplementedOnCuda { op: 'einsum_general' }) because the four-way classification (batch/free_a/free_b/contract) assumes distinct chars per operand. Closing this requires a pre-pass that diagonalises the offending operand(s) via the same as_strided_copy machinery added in #821, then feeds the reduced operand into the general decomposition. Filed as a sub-cascade so it can be addressed independently.
